# Audit checklist

This document tracks every static-analysis and invariant-test finding raised against the Helios Solidity surface, and how each finding has been resolved (mitigated, accepted as known limitation, or queued for fix). It is the human-side gate for the Phase 6 security pass ÔÇö `.github/workflows/security.yml` is informational only and never blocks a merge.

**Scope.** All Solidity in `contracts/src/` plus the autogenerated snarkjs Groth16 verifiers in `contracts/src/verifiers/`. Third-party libraries (`contracts/lib/`), tests, deploy scripts, and mocks are filtered out of automated scans (see `contracts/slither.config.json`).

**Audience.** Helios maintainers running pre-release security passes, plus external auditors performing a one-shot review. Each row is meant to give a reviewer enough context to decide whether the triage decision was reasonable without re-reading the contract.

**Threat-model framework.** Severity assignments tie back to the trust assumptions and threat scenarios in `Helios.md ┬º15`. Where a finding maps cleanly onto a documented threat, the **Mitigation** column references the relevant subsection.

**Status legend.**

- **Accepted** ÔÇö known limitation, behaviour is intentional, no code change required. Reviewer rationale recorded inline.
- **Mitigated** ÔÇö code-level defence already prevents the failure mode (gating modifier, reentrancy guard, validation, etc.).
- **Fixed** ÔÇö finding addressed by a specific commit / PR (linked in Mitigation).
- **Open** ÔÇö accepted onto the backlog; not blocking v1.
- **False positive** ÔÇö detector misread; explanation inline.

---

## Slither

Last run: 2026-05-08, against commit at HEAD of `main` (Phase 5 cross-chain shipped). Run reproduced locally with `slither-analyzer 0.11.5` and `solc 0.8.28`. CI re-runs on every pull request via `.github/workflows/security.yml`.

Aggregate counts: **9 High, 27 Medium, 57 Low, 227 Informational** (320 findings total). The High and Medium tables below are exhaustive (deduplicated to one row per detector ├ù call-site cluster). Low and Informational findings are not enumerated here ÔÇö they are dominated by `naming-convention` (195 of them, almost all in the autogenerated verifiers) and `timestamp` lint on the auto-defund freshness checks; both classes are reviewed in bulk and rolled forward unless a specific entry is escalated.

### High severity

All 9 High findings are `incorrect-return` in the snarkjs-generated Groth16 verifiers. These are well-known false positives in `snarkjs@0.7.6`'s template: the assembly blocks intentionally `return(0, 0x20)` on field-element validation failure, which Slither cannot distinguish from an unexpected halt. The verifier outputs are fixed for any given circuit and are validated end-to-end by circuit unit tests (`circuits/Makefile` ÔåÆ `make test`) plus the `TradeAttestationVerifier` integration tests. Replacing them is a snarkjs version-pin migration, not a code-fix (`MEMORY.md ÔåÆ snarkjs pinned to exact 0.7.6`).

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SL-H-01 | `verifiers/MomentumV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (`asm_0.checkField`, `checkPairing`, `g1_mulAccC`) ÔÇö 3 sites | High | False positive | Autogenerated by snarkjs 0.7.6. Halts (`return(0, 0x20)`) are intentional field-validation failures. End-to-end correctness is covered by `circuits/test` proof-generation tests + on-chain integration tests in `contracts/test/TradeAttestationVerifier.t.sol`. |
| SL-H-02 | `verifiers/MeanReversionV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (same 3 sites) | High | False positive | Same template + same mitigation as SL-H-01. |
| SL-H-03 | `verifiers/YieldRotationV1Verifier.sol` | `incorrect-return` in `verifyProof` (same 3 sites) | High | False positive | Same template + same mitigation as SL-H-01. |

### Medium severity

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| SL-M-01 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `allocateToStrategy` ÔÇö state writes (`rec.strategy`, `rec.capitalDeployed`, `_userTotalDeployed`) follow external calls into `IUserVaultForAllocator.transferToAllocator` and `IStrategyVault.allocateFrom`. | Medium | Mitigated | Function carries `nonReentrant` (line 232, ReentrancyGuardTransient). External callees are vault contracts owned by Helios and gated by `onlyAllocatorVault` modifiers; they cannot call back into `AllocatorVault`. Slither does not model the gating. |
| SL-M-02 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_allocateInternal` (used by `allocateToStrategy` and `rebalance`) | Medium | Mitigated | Same as SL-M-01. Both public entry points are `nonReentrant`. |
| SL-M-03 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_withdrawPartial` | Medium | Mitigated | Internal helper called only from `rebalance` (`nonReentrant`, line 467) and `defundStrategy` (`nonReentrant`, line 288). |
| SL-M-04 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `_unwindAndCredit` | Medium | Mitigated | Internal helper called only from `defundStrategy`, `finalizeDefund`, both `nonReentrant`. |
| SL-M-05 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `defundStrategy` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 288). External callees are Helios-controlled vaults. |
| SL-M-06 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `finalizeDefund` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 388). |
| SL-M-07 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `rebalance` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 467). |
| SL-M-08 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `reentrancy-no-eth` in `settleStrategyFee` | Medium | Mitigated | `nonReentrant` (line 524). |
| SL-M-09 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `settleStrategyFee` (`realizedTotal == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Strict equality on `uint256` returned by `distributeRealized` is intentional ÔÇö a return of exactly zero means "nothing realized this epoch", and the function early-returns to skip HWM update. Cannot underflow because `realizedTotal` is a fresh return value, not an accumulator. |
| SL-M-10 | `OraclePriceAnchor.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `latest()` (`n == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | `n` is the post-incremented commit counter; a zero check is the canonical "no commits yet" sentinel and matches the contract's invariant that commit IDs start at 1. |
| SL-M-11 | `OracleYieldAnchor.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `latest()` (`n == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Same pattern as SL-M-10. |
| SL-M-12 | `StrategyRegistry.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `initiateParamsRotation` (`_paramsHashOf[strategyId] == bytes32(0)`) | Medium | Accepted | Used to gate "strategy must already be registered before its params can rotate." Zero is the deliberate unregistered sentinel. |
| SL-M-13 | `StrategyRegistry.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `completeParamsRotation` (`p.newHash == bytes32(0)`) | Medium | Accepted | Same pattern; zero indicates "no rotation pending." |
| SL-M-14 | `StrategyVault.sol` | `incorrect-equality` in `_checkNavDivergence` (`markedFloor == 0`) | Medium | Accepted | Intentional early-return when the cash-floor check has no floor to compare against (zero deployed). Maps to the ┬º6.4 cash-floor invariant in `Helios.md`. |
| SL-M-15 | `UserVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` ÔÇö `found` flag in `setMetaStrategy`, `_isAddressSubset`, `_isBytes32Subset`, `_isUint32Subset` (4 sites) | Medium | Accepted | Solidity zero-initialises stack variables; the boolean flags rely on the default `false`. Idiomatic pattern for subset-check helpers. Slither warns by category; behaviour is defined. |
| SL-M-16 | `AllocatorVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` ÔÇö `reopening` (in `allocateToStrategy` and `_allocateInternal`), `sum`, `totalDeployed` (in `rebalance`) | Medium | Accepted | Same as SL-M-15: integers and bools default to zero / false. |
| SL-M-17 | `AllocatorRegistry.sol` | `uninitialized-local` ÔÇö loop counter `j` in `_nameKey` | Medium | Accepted | Default-zero loop counter; idiomatic. |
| SL-M-18 | `StrategyVault.sol` | `uninitialized-local` ÔÇö `data` in `_forwardAttestationIfRemote` | Medium | Accepted | `bytes memory` initialises empty by default, which is the intended state when no remote forward is needed. |
| SL-M-19 | `verifiers/MomentumV1VerifierAdapter.sol`, `MeanReversionV1VerifierAdapter.sol`, `YieldRotationV1VerifierAdapter.sol` | `uninitialized-local` ÔÇö `fixedInputs` in `verifyProof` (3 sites) | Medium | Accepted | Adapter copies `pubInputs` into a fixed-size memory array; the array is fully written before being passed to the underlying verifier. False positive in Slither's flow analysis. |

### Low / Informational

The 57 Low and 227 Informational findings break down as:

- 195 `naming-convention` ÔÇö all in the snarkjs-generated verifiers (`asm_0`, `g1_mulAccC`, etc.). Cannot be renamed without diverging from the generator output.
- 23 `timestamp` ÔÇö `block.timestamp` reads on auto-defund freshness, oracle staleness, fee crystallisation. Threshold values (180s freshness, 5% defund) make sub-second drift irrelevant. See `Helios.md ┬º6.10`, ┬º6.4.
- 12 `assembly` ÔÇö verifier templates, `MetaStrategyLib` Poseidon helper. Reviewed during Phase 2 circuit/contract integration.
- 10 `calls-loop` ÔÇö controlled bounded loops over user-allocations and registered strategies. Bound enforced by `MAX_STRATEGIES` constant in `AllocatorVault`.
- 7 `unused-state` ÔÇö kept-for-upgradeability storage gaps in UUPS proxies. Intentional.
- 7 `missing-zero-check` ÔÇö owner-set addresses (oracle anchor, fee receiver). Owner is the Helios multi-sig; treated as trusted (`Helios.md ┬º15.1`).
- 7 `reentrancy-benign` / 7 `reentrancy-events` ÔÇö same `nonReentrant`-gated paths as SL-M-01..08, just lower-severity event-ordering variants.
- 5 `missing-inheritance` ÔÇö interfaces declared inline rather than extending. Stylistic, not a defect.
- 4 `cyclomatic-complexity` ÔÇö `AllocatorVault.rebalance`, `StrategyVault._postTrade`. Acknowledged tradeoff for keeping the fee/HWM logic in one transaction.
- 3 `events-maths` ÔÇö accumulator-only updates that don't emit (e.g. internal HWM tracking). Subgraph reads from the parent event; no consumer needs the per-step.
- 2 `unindexed-event-address` ÔÇö minor; will fix in next ABI bump.
- 1 `solc-version` ÔÇö pragma `^0.8.28` is the current Foundry pin; not changing during Phase 6.
- 1 `low-level-calls` ÔÇö LayerZero OApp callback invocation; required by the OApp spec.

If any of these escalates during external audit, promote it to its own row in the table above with a status update.

---

## Mythril

Mythril is **not** wired into CI for the reasons documented in `.github/workflows/security.yml` (slow on `via_ir` builds, high overlap with Slither's reentrancy detector, runs frequently OOM on GitHub-hosted runners). Instead, Helios maintainers run it manually against the three vault contracts before each release tag.

### Pip procedure (works on a dev box with `python3-dev` headers + outbound network)

```bash
pip install mythril==0.24.8
forge build
myth analyze src/AllocatorVault.sol --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12 --solc-json mythril.solc.json
myth analyze src/UserVault.sol      --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12 --solc-json mythril.solc.json
myth analyze src/StrategyVault.sol  --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12 --solc-json mythril.solc.json
```

`mythril.solc.json` (checked into `contracts/`) carries the Foundry remappings + `viaIR: true` + `evmVersion: cancun` + the production optimizer settings, so Mythril's invocation matches what `forge build` produces.

### Docker procedure (no Python headers needed; works in restricted environments)

```bash
cd contracts
# Stage solc 0.8.28 (foundry installs it under ~/.svm/0.8.28/) so the
# container doesn't need outbound network to fetch it.
STAGE=$(mktemp -d)
cp ~/.svm/0.8.28/solc-0.8.28 "$STAGE/solc-v0.8.28" && chmod 755 "$STAGE/solc-v0.8.28" && chmod 755 "$STAGE"

for V in src/UserVault.sol src/AllocatorVault.sol src/StrategyVault.sol ; do
  docker run --rm \
    -v "$(pwd):/work" -v "$STAGE:/staged-solc:ro" -w /work \
    --entrypoint sh \
    mythril/myth:latest \
    -c "mkdir -p /home/mythril/.solcx \
        && cp /staged-solc/solc-v0.8.28 /home/mythril/.solcx/ \
        && chmod +x /home/mythril/.solcx/solc-v0.8.28 \
        && myth analyze $V --solv 0.8.28 --execution-timeout 600 --max-depth 12 --solc-json mythril.solc.json"
done
```

### Last run

**2026-05-12 ÔÇö clean.** Mythril v0.24.8 via the docker procedure above against `UserVault`, `AllocatorVault`, `StrategyVault` at the HEAD of `main` (post-cross-decimal cutover). Solc 0.8.28 staged from `~/.svm/`; remappings + `viaIR: true` + `evmVersion: cancun` + `optimizer.runs: 200` supplied via `contracts/mythril.solc.json` to match the production build.

| Contract | Execution timeout | Max depth | Outcome |
|---|---|---|---|
| `src/UserVault.sol` | 300 s | 8 | The analysis was completed successfully. No issues were detected. |
| `src/AllocatorVault.sol` | 600 s | 12 | The analysis was completed successfully. No issues were detected. |
| `src/StrategyVault.sol` | 600 s | 12 | The analysis was completed successfully. No issues were detected. |

`via_ir = true` did not trip Mythril when settings were passed via `--solc-json` ÔÇö the historical flakiness documented in `.github/workflows/security.yml` appears to bite only the default-args invocation path.

| ID | Contract | Finding | Severity | Status | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| _(none)_ | | | | | Mythril 0.24.8 clean on all three vaults at HEAD of `main` (2026-05-12). |


---

## Foundry invariants

Property tests live in `contracts/test/invariant/` and `contracts/test/*Invariant.t.sol`. They run on every PR via the `contracts` job in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`forge test -vv`) and gate merge through the 85% line-coverage check (raised to 90% before submission, per `CLAUDE.md`).

Captured invariants:

| ID | Contract | Invariant | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| INV-01 | `UserVault` | `sum(allocations[user]) Ôëñ deposits[user]` for every user, every block | Holds | Enforced by `_checkAllocationCap` on every `allocateToStrategy` call; tested via stateful invariant fuzz in `contracts/test/UserVault.t.sol`. |
| INV-02 | `AllocatorVault` | `_userTotalDeployed[user] == ╬ú allocations[user][s].capitalDeployed` | Holds | Enforced after every allocate/withdraw/defund by paired bookkeeping; ghost variable in invariant test cross-checks every step. |
| INV-03 | `AllocatorVault` | Once `defundedAt > 0`, `capitalDeployed == 0` until the slot is reopened by a fresh `allocateToStrategy` | Holds | `_unwindAndCredit` zeros `capitalDeployed` and `_allocateInternal` resets `defundedAt` when `reopening` is true. |
| INV-04 | `StrategyVault` | `cashHeld + outstandingPositions ÔëÑ markedNAV ├ù (1 - NAV_DIVERGENCE_THRESHOLD_BPS/10_000)` (┬º6.4 cash-floor invariant) | Holds | Enforced by `_checkNavDivergence` on every `reportNAV`; second consecutive breach emits `NavDivergenceObserved`. |
| INV-05 | `TradeAttestationVerifier` | A trade attestation only verifies if `(declaredClass, paramsHash, oracleRoot)` triple matches a `(strategyId, registeredClass)` row in `StrategyRegistry` | Holds | Enforced by `_validateAttestation` in `verifyAndAttest`; tested in `contracts/test/TradeAttestationVerifier.t.sol`. |
| INV-06 | `OraclePriceAnchor` / `OracleYieldAnchor` | `freshness(root)` reverts after 180s; `revokeRoot` makes a previously-valid root revert immediately | Holds | Phase-3 redeploy added `freshness()` and `unrevokeRoot()` (HIGH #6/#9); see `CLAUDE.md ÔåÆ Key addresses` for the redeployed pin. |
| INV-07 | `ReputationAnchor` (V1, registry-bound) | Only the registered signer can post an update; signature is bound to `(strategyId, epoch, score, componentsHash)` | Holds | EIP-712 signature verification on every post; signer registry change is multi-sig. |

Add new invariants here as Phase 6 polish surfaces them. Each row should link the invariant to either a ┬º6 contract clause or a ┬º15.2 threat-model entry in `Helios.md`.

---

## Maintenance

- The Slither table is regenerated by re-running `slither contracts/ --filter-paths "lib/|test/|script/|mocks/"` from the repo root. The CI workflow uploads the same report on every PR; the table below it should track diffs, not the raw output.
- Promote a Low/Informational finding to its own row only when a reviewer is asked to look at it specifically ÔÇö otherwise the rolled-up summary above is the canonical record.
- Mythril rows land in the table immediately after a manual run; record the symbolic execution timeout used and the contract address (mainnet / testnet).
- Foundry invariant rows are append-only ÔÇö once an invariant holds and is in CI, it stays in this document even if the corresponding code is refactored, so regressions in future releases are easy to attribute.
